feedback.testWarning: This is a test environment for preview purposes. Data can be deleted at any time.

home.title: Reciprocal Reviews
call: Academia incentivizes publishing over peer review. That means too many submissions and not enough reviews, or reviews that are late and low quality.
1. **Editors set a cost** for submissions, priced in review tokens.
2. **Authors earn tokens** by volunteering for venues, sharing expertise and availability, and submitting on-time, quality reviews, on whatever reviewing platform a venue uses.

3. **Authors spend tokens** to submit manuscripts for review, splitting the cost with co-authors. Or, they can gift tokens to individuals or shared pools to strategically support newcomers, colleagues in crisis, or emerging topics.

Because authors need to submit high-quality, on-time reviews to submit, authors get better, faster reviews, editors do less nagging, communities overall have a fairer distribution of peer review labor.

tip.browse: **Interested in adopting?** See the [journals and conferences](/venues) using the platform and reach out to their editors for their experiences. Or, [log in](/login) to propose a journal or conference adopt *Reciprocal Reviews*.
tip.about: **We are a volunteer community** of academics, not a for-profit organization. Our only goal is to improve the sustainability, quality, and integrity of peer review. See our [about](/about) page if you're interested in helping.
tip.track: **Don't want to set a cost?** Use Reciprocal Reviews to track volunteer reviewers and expertise in your community, streamlining reviewing and program committee invitations.

feedback.beta: We're in development! A public beta is likely in 2026. Visit [GitHub](https://github.com/reciprocalreviews) to see who's involved, see our [beta milestone](https://github.com/reciprocalreviews/reciprocalapp/issues?q=is:open%20milestone:%22Beta%22) for the work that remains. Subscribe to our low-volume [newsletter](https://reciprocalreviews.substack.com/) to follow along, check the [updates](/updates) page to see our weekly progress. And if you feel like helping, email us! We're a small volunteer group of academics.

paragraph.intro:
Ready to create a new submission? Typically, this form is filled by one of the authors of a submission, to pay for a submission to be reviewed. After filling out this form:

- The submission will be created in the system.
- All scholars listed will need to confirm their payments.
- Once everyone has paid, the editor will proceed with review.

feedback.viewSettings: Welcome admin! Check the [settings](/venue/{venue}/settings) page to edit venue compensation, roles, and anonymity.

setup.note: Steps to get your venue ready for volunteers and submissions

paragraph.submissionTypes: These are the submission types for this venue. Each role can have a different amount of compensation for each type (e.g., reviews of new submissions may be compensated more than re-reviews).
paragraph.missingCompensation: Are you **missing compensation** for a role you accepted? It's possible it hasn't been entered by the scholar responsible. You can request compensation for it here, and the scholar responsible will be notified.

feedback.logIn: [Log in](/login) to view this venue's settings.
feedback.adminsOnly: Only admins of this venue can view this page.

inactiveMessage.label: inactive message
inactiveMessage.placeholder: e.g., We're currently configuring the venue.

submissionCost.label: submission cost (price to submit a manuscript to the venue)

checkbox.inactive: **Inactive venue**: Scholars cannot volunteer, submit, or otherwise interact with this venue.

anonymousAssignments.on: **Anonymous assignments**: Authors and conflicted scholars cannot see who is assigned to review their submissions.
anonymousAssignments.off: **Visible assignments**: Authors and conflicted scholars can see who is assigned to review their submissions. This is open reviewing.

paragraph.welcome: Welcome venue admin! You can manage this venue's settings here.
paragraph.setupIntro: Hello venue admin! Are you ready to integrate into your reviewing system? There are a few steps:
paragraph.setupSteps:
1. Update the inactive message in the settings below, so your community knows you're busy configuring things.
2. Run a community process to decide on all settings below.
3. Include this venue's [payment link](/venue/{venueid}/submissions/new) in author instructions and submission confirmations, prompting authors to pay after submission. If you want the manuscript ID to be populated automatically and your reviewing platform supports it, you can use the URL `https://reciprocal.reviews/venue/{venueid}/submission/new?id=[ID]`, but replace `[ID]` with the variable your system uses for manuscript ID.
4. Update your reviewing platform's **review submission** notification email to prompt the scholar receiving it with a link to the submission: `https://reciprocal.reviews/venue/{venueid}/submission/[id]`, but replace `[id]` with the variable your system uses for manuscript ID. In the email, prompt them to add the reviewer to the submission (if they haven't already), evaluate the review, and if it meets your venue's review quality requirements, press the **Complete** button to pay for their work.
5. When you're ready to launch, set the venue to active in the **Status** section below, and the venue will be open for volunteering.

tip.inactive: Check the inactive message in the settings below, so your community knows you're busy configuring things.
tip.compensation: It is critical that you think carefully about the compensation structure for your venue. Giving too many welcome tokens disincentivizes reviewing, but not giving enough can deter newcomers from contributring.
tip.roles: Configure your venue's volunteer roles below, create roles such as **reviewer**, **program commitee**, **associate editor** to represent the different kinds of contributions volunteers can make to this venue. Ensure you set total compensation levels for each role to not be higher than the cost of a submission.

tip.mintWarning: Be careful creating new tokens. Too many tokens per scholar will diminish the incentive to earn them.

note.minters: Minters can see, approve, and cancel transactions, and most importantly, mint new tokens in this currency. They can also propose and improve currency exchanges and mergers.

mintPurpose.label: why are these tokens being minted? This purpose will be public in the transaction history.

minter.label: scholar
minter.invalidMinter: Minters can't be admins of a venue that uses this currency.
minter.invalidContact: Must be a valid email or ORCID

mint.note: Create new tokens in this currency.

addMinter.note: Allow another scholar to mint tokens.

checkbox.mintConsent: I understand that creating excess tokens will erode this currency's value.

tokenOwner.label: which venue should own the new tokens?

paragraph.mintersDescription: These scholars are the minters for this currency. They can see and approve all transactions.
paragraph.venuesDescription: These are the venues that use this currency:

paragraph.community: We are a volunteer community of academics interested in keeping peer review sustainable, for ourselves and our communities. You can learn more about who's contributing and how to contribute yourself on [GitHub](https://github.com/reciprocalreviews).
paragraph.stewardsIntro: Stewards oversee the platform and its use, guiding design, implementation, and usage with the goal of ensuring the platform is improving the sustainability of academic peer review:
paragraph.joinStewards: Would you like to become a steward? Reach out to any of the stewards above and let's chat.
paragraph.theoryIntro: The theory of change behind this platform is rests upon the following argument:
paragraph.closing: We built this platform to test the claims above, and hope you'll join us in this effort!

paragraph.intro: Here are the updates to Reciprocal Reviews. Have a [defect](https://github.com/reciprocalreviews/reciprocalapp/issues/new?template=1-defect.yml) to report or [improvement](https://github.com/reciprocalreviews/reciprocalapp/issues/new?template=2-improvement.yml) to suggest?

tip.highestPriority: This is the highest priority role. Any volunteers for this role are automatically assigned this role for new submissions.

paragraph.adminsDescription: Admins can edit venue information, add and remove other admins, and gift tokens. They are typically Editors-in-Chief, Program Chairs of a conference, or editorial staff.
